A: Letter A in Indian Sign Language — closed fist with thumb on the side.
B: Letter B — flat hand, fingers together and upright.
C: Letter C — hand forms the shape of the letter C.
D: Letter D — index finger up, thumb touching middle finger.
E: Letter E — curled fingers touching thumb.
F: Letter F — circle made with thumb and index finger, others extended.
G: Letter G — index and thumb parallel, pointing sideways.
H: Letter H — two fingers pointing sideways.
J: Letter J — trace letter J with pinky finger.
K: Letter K — index and middle finger extended forming V, thumb between.
L: Letter L — index finger up, thumb extended sideways.
M: Letter M — thumb under first three fingers.
N: Letter N — thumb under first two fingers.
O: Letter O — make a circle with fingers.
P: Letter P — similar to K but downward.
Q: Letter Q — similar to G but pointing down.
R: Letter R — index and middle fingers crossed.
S: Letter S — closed fist, thumb across front.
T: Letter T — thumb between index and middle fingers.
U: Letter U — index and middle fingers together.
V: Letter V — index and middle fingers form a V shape.
X: Letter X — bent index finger pointing up.
Y: Letter Y — thumb and pinky out, other fingers folded.
Z: Letter Z — trace Z shape in air with index finger.
